rulings.Case Title: CENTER FOR ENVIRONMENTAL HEALTH, A NON-PROFIT CORP VS. MICHAELS STORES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Center For Environmental Health, A Non-Profit Corp.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Approval Of Settlement And Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Novtex Corporation, Jo-Ann Sotres, Inc., And Hancock Fabrics, Inc. (Health & Safety Code 25249.7 (F))
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 7, 2007, LINE 1. PLAINTIFF ENVIROMENTAL HEALTH, CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR COURT APPROVAL OF SETTLEMENT AND ENTRY OF CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO NOVTEX CORPORATION, JO-ANN SOTRES, INC. AND HANCOCK FABRICS, INC. (HEALTH & SAFETY CODE SECTION 25249.7 (F)): HEARING REQUIRED. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF NEEDS TO SUPPLEMENT THE RECORD WITH ADDITIONAL FACTS TO JUSTIFY THE FEE REQUEST, INCLUDING: THE EXPERIENCE OF THE LAWYERS, THE TIME SPENT ON PARTICULAR TASKS AND COMPARABLE MARKET RATES. AS TO C.E.H., THE COURT NEEDS TO CONFIRM THAT ITS EMPLOYEES HAVE NO PERSONAL OR BUSINESS RELATIONSHIP WITH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND MORE DETAIL ON JUST WHAT AND HOW MUCH C.E.H. SPENDS ON OTHER PUBLIC PROJECTS. =(302/PJM/CK)

rulings.Case Title: ELIO ANTONIO VALLE VS. UNIVISION RADIO, INC., A CALIFORNIA BUSINESS ENTIT et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 7, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF ELIO VALLE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS OFF CALENDAR, PER JUNE 21, 2007 DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: J.R. MARKETING, LLC., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Deft To Strike Portions Of Pltfs' Second Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 7, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANT HARTFORD CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY'S Motion To Strike Portions Of PLAINTIFFS' Second Amended Complaint IS DENIED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT COMPLIES WITH THE COURT'S PRIOR ORDER. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACTS THAT SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH C.C.P. SECTION 128.7(C). =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN BANGO VS. MAURYNE LEES et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 7, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANT BLACKHAWK CLAIMS SERVICE, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS TO PLEAD FACTS AS TO WHEN PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM ACCRUED. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: JANET METH VS. RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, AUGUST 7, 2007, LINE 8. PETITIONER JANET METH'S Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandamus IS DENIED. C.C.P. SECTION 170.1 (A)(6)(A)(iii) DOES NOT APPLY TO ADMINISTRATIVE HEARING OFFICERS. GAI V CITY OF SELMAN (1998) 68 CAL. APP 4TH 213. 229-233. IF THE PROVISION WERE APPLICABLE, PLAINTIFF DELAYED IN SEEKING THIS WRIT. GAI AT 230. PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO SHOW ACTUAL BIAS TOWARD PLAINTIFF (ANDREWS V AGRICULTURAL LABOR RELATIONS (1981) 28 CAL 3D 781, 794) AND HAS NOT DONE SO. =(302/PJM/KD)
